content: I'm so excited for my upcoming performance in La Bohème at the Vienna State Opera on March 15th. I've been reading a lot of books on opera history to get into character.
That sounds like a fantastic experience! What kind of books have you been reading to prepare for your role?

I've been enjoying 'The Opera Singer's Guide to Vocal Technique' and 'A History of Opera' by Donald Grout. I also just finished reading 'The Name of the Rose' by Umberto Eco, which was a great distraction from my rehearsals.
Those books sound like great resources. I've heard 'The Name of the Rose' is a fascinating novel. Have you had a chance to explore Vienna during your downtime?
Yes, I have! I love walking along the Danube Canal and visiting the gardens at Schönbrunn Palace. It's been great to have my golden retriever, Willow, with me to explore the city.
That sounds like a lovely way to spend your free time. How's Willow adjusting to all the travel and new environments?
She's doing great, thanks for asking! I've been trying to find dog-friendly cafes and parks in each city we visit. It's been a fun challenge to balance my performances with taking care of her.
I can imagine. It's great that you're prioritizing Willow's needs. Do you have any favorite opera roles or performances that you've been a part of in the past?

I loved performing as Carmen at the Sydney Opera House last year. The energy of the audience was electric! I'm also a big fan of cooking Thai food in my free time, and I've been trying out new recipes in each city we visit.
That sounds like an amazing experience. I've heard the Sydney Opera House is a stunning venue. What's your favorite Thai dish to cook?
I'm a big fan of making green curry with chicken and vegetables. I've also been trying to learn more about mushroom foraging, and I've found some great spots in the forests around Vienna.
Mmm, that sounds delicious! Mushroom foraging is such a unique hobby. I'll have to look into that for future conversation topics.
